public_title: Randomised trial of metformin as chemoprevention in oral potentially malignant disorders (OPMDs) in tobacco users in India
scientific_title: Phase II randomised placebo-controlled trial of metformin 500 mg BD versus placebo for 12 months in adults with oral leukoplakia or oral submucous fibrosis who use tobacco

intervention.name: Metformin 500 mg twice daily for 12 months
intervention.details: Metformin hydrochloride 500 mg BID orally with meals; dose escalated to 1000 mg BID after 4 weeks if tolerated

comparator.name: Matched placebo twice daily for 12 months

inclusion_criteria: Adults aged 20 to 60 years; Clinically and/or histologically confirmed oral leukoplakia or oral submucous fibrosis; Current tobacco user (smoking or smokeless) for at least 5 years; No diabetes mellitus; Fasting blood glucose <126 mg/dL; Adequate renal function (eGFR ≥60 mL/min/1.73m²); Willingness to attend monthly follow-up visits; Signed informed consent
exclusion_criteria: Established diagnosis of diabetes mellitus or on anti-diabetic medication; Lactic acidosis risk factors (severe hepatic disease, alcoholism, CHF); Prior or current malignancy of any site; Pregnancy or breastfeeding; Use of systemic retinoids, corticosteroids, or other chemoprevention agents in last 6 months; Allergy to metformin

sites.name: Maulana Azad Institute of Dental Sciences
